mortality.systemic_cause: asphyxiation accounts for 100% of deaths — systemic infrastructure failure

insights.title: Most stable governance: direct_democracy
insights.evidence: direct_democracy lasted 85 years (years 16-end), weathering 16 crises with 99% average cohesion
insights.amendment_text: Any governance proposal affecting more than 3 agents must first be modeled in a sandboxed sub-simulation.  The simulation results become part of the proposal's public record.  Governance decisions without simulation evidence are advisory only.  The most stable governance model (direct_democracy lasted 85 years (years 16-end), weathering 16 crises with 99% average cohesion) should be the default reversion target when experimental governance fails.

insights.title: Sub-simulations are predictive, not just justificatory
insights.evidence: Sub-sims achieved 75% accuracy in predicting resource outcomes — better than chance (50%)

insights.title: Dominant faction: The Resolute
insights.evidence: 9 colonists aligned around resolve with 94% cohesion

insights.title: Single-point-of-failure infrastructure kills colonies
insights.evidence: asphyxiation accounts for 100% of deaths — systemic infrastructure failure

insights.title: Governance changes follow death
insights.evidence: 1 of 2 governance phases had deaths during their tenure. Mortality is the primary driver of political change.

The simulation results become part of the proposal's public record.  Governance decisions without simulation evidence are advisory only.
